other_observers: None, but the bird was seen by numerous other observers. I was not original "finder" of the bird but sought it based on posts on Cobirds.;
description: The main issue was to differentiate this bird from other phalaropes. The bird was distant but I spent a great deal of time looking for what I considered to be definitive field marks. From the expected species, Wilson's: a much shorter, heavier, more blunt bill. Clearly seen. From Red-necked: pale uniform unmarked gray back, and much thinner bill.Behaviors: Floating out on open water, and the bird was by itself.Call: N/APlumage: basic
